description: Inside an apartment, Joey and Chandler squat near a television stand. Joey holds a small yellow chick in his hands. A white duck and several sculpted head bookends sit on the floor in the foreground. Joey looks at the chick.
Joey says, "Hey, wouldn't it be cool if our duck and our chick had a little baby? We'd call it Chuck" Chandler looks at Joey.
Joey smiles at the chick while Chandler continues looking at him.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "or Dick." Joey laughs.
Ross enters through the background door, wearing a red long-sleeved shirt and light-colored pants.
Ross walks forward. Joey and Chandler say, "Hey," as Ross approaches.
Ross stops and moves his hands upward.
Ross says, "listen, I need a favor," gesturing slightly. Joey and Chandler stand up.
Joey and Chandler stand facing Ross.
Ross says, "I was in the shower, and as I was cleansing myself," keeping his arms mostly at his sides.
Ross pauses, looking at Chandler.
Ross continues, "well, I felt something." Chandler asks, "Was it like a sneeze, only better?"
Ross looks at Chandler with a neutral facial expression.
Ross says, "No, I mean like a thing on my body." Joey and Chandler look at him.
Joey looks steadily at Ross.
Joey asks, "What was it?" Ross replies, "Well, I don't know. It's kind of in a place that's"
Ross continues looking between Joey and Chandler.
Ross says, "not visually accessible to me. And I was hoping maybe you guys could help me out." Joey leans back slightly and asks, "Why?"
Ross begins turning his back to them.
Ross stands with his back to them, pointing backward over his shoulder, and says, "Come on, you guys. No big deal."
Ross says, "Just"
Ross bends forward at the waist, presenting his lower back to Joey and Chandler. They lean forward to inspect his back.
Joey looks closely and asks, "what is it? Is it a mole?"
Chandler and Joey continue inspecting Ross's back as he stays bent over.
Chandler says, "No, it's too wrinkly to be a mole."
Joey wrinkles his face and asks, "EW. Is it? What, a pimple?"
Ross remains bent over while Joey and Chandler stare at his back.
Ross, still bent over, says, "No, it's fancier than a pimple." Chandler places a hand on Ross's back.
Chandler says, "Look, Ross, why don't you just go see a duck," Rachel opens the background door.
Rachel stands in the doorway. Ross abruptly stands up straight and turns around. Chandler and Joey step back.
Chandler looks at Ross and says, "okay, well, it's definite. Two more weeks of winter."
Chandler remains looking at Ross. The view turns black.
The setting changes to an exterior daytime view of city buildings.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Chandler and Rachel sit on a sofa reading, while Joey sits on a chair holding a mug. Phoebe walks in with Vince, a man in a green polo shirt.
Phoebe points around the room and says, "Okay? And then this is the coffee house. This is where I play my music."
Vince says, "Good deal." Phoebe continues, "Yeah. And these are my friends. People. This"
Phoebe gestures to Vince and says, "is Vince. Vince, the people."
Vince waves slightly and says, "Hi."
Chandler raises his mug while Joey waves in greeting.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Great. Vince is a fireman."
Rachel raises her eyebrows and asks, "Wow. Have you ever rescued anyone from a burning building before?"
Vince replies, "98 hot saves. Highest on the force."
Chandler raises his hands in a double thumbs-up gesture and says, "Well, you know, if Joey and I play with matches we could get you up to an even hundred."
Vince looks steadily at Chandler with a neutral facial expression.
Vince says, "Fire safety is not a joke, son."
Chandler slowly lowers his thumbs.
Chandler drops his hands and says, "You're right. I know."
Vince turns his attention back to Phoebe.
Vince looks at Phoebe and says, "Look, I gotta go. I'm on call tonight. See you Saturday." Phoebe replies, "Okay."
Vince turns and walks out. Phoebe smiles as she watches him leave.
Rachel asks, "Wow, he's cute. Phoebes, but I thought you just started dating the kindergarten teacher." Phoebe replies, "Oh, Jason?"
Phoebe says, "Yeah, we're seeing each other tonight."
Rachel turns fully toward Phoebe.
Rachel asks, "Phoebe, two dates in one day? That's so unlike you."
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she says, "I know. I'm, like, playing the,"
She continues, "like juggling two guys and sewing my wild"
Phoebe pauses, moving her hands in circular motions.
Phoebe continues, "know, this kind of know oat sewing field playing juggler."
Joey leans forward from his chair and asks, "So Phoebes. Do they know about each other?" Phoebe replies, "Does the dog's lips move when he reads?"
Joey keeps his gaze on Phoebe with a static facial expression.
Phoebe shakes her head slightly and says, "Okay, no, they don't know."
Phoebe looks away, while Gunther works behind the counter in the background.
Ross walks in from the right and says, "Hey, guys." The group responds, "Hey."
Ross walks behind the sofa.
In a coffee shop, Ross, wearing an orange sweater, stands at the counter next to Joey and Chandler. Chandler is seated.
Joey turns to Ross and asks, "Well, okay. How'd it go with the doctors?"
Ross gestures with both hands and replies, "Well, he said there's definitely nothing to worry about. It's totally benign."
Joey asks, "What is he they"
Ross continues, gesturing with his hands, "couldn't even tell me. He said it was just some sort of skin abnormality."
Ross lowers his hands and looks at Joey.
Joey asks, "So it's just skin?"
Ross replies, "Well, no, he said it's skin like"
Chandler, seated at the counter, asks, "okay, so it's not skin?"
Ross gestures with one hand and says, "Well, it's in the skin family."
Ross looks from Chandler to Joey.
Ross says, "And the worst thing is, he said without being able to identify it he was reluctant to remove it."
Chandler leans on the counter and says, "Well, you know"
Chandler gestures towards himself and says, "what? You should go to my guy. Because when I went in there with my third nipple"
Chandler moves his hand in a slicing motion.
Chandler says, "he just lopped it right off. So I guess I'm lucky."
Chandler looks down slightly, rubbing his chin, and adds, "I mean, not as lucky as people who were born with two nipples." Ross looks at Chandler.
Joey turns to Ross.
Joey gestures toward Ross and says, "At least they knew what yours was. Yours had a name."
Joey keeps his gaze on Ross.
Joey points at Ross and says, "Oh, maybe they'll name yours after"
Joey pauses.
Joey says, "you."
Joey lightly hits Ross's shoulder and says, "They'd call it the Ross."
Joey continues, "And then people would be like, oh, he's got a Ross." Joey smiles as Ross looks at him with a static facial expression.
Chandler looks forward.
Chandler says, "Yeah, that'd be cool."
The scene changes to Monica walking up to Rachel and Phoebe, who are sitting on a couch in the coffee shop.
Monica stands near the couch and says, "Pete's breaking up with me."
Rachel, turning toward Monica, asks, "What?"
Monica gestures with her hands.
Monica says, "I just checked my messages, and he said that when he gets back from Atlanta, we need to talk."
Rachel asks, "And?"
Monica gestures and replies, "Well, that's it. People never say, we need to talk unless it's something bad."
Joey walks over and says, "Whoa. That doesn't necessarily mean he's breaking"
Joey continues, "up with you."
Monica asks, "Really?" Joey replies, "Yeah."
Joey shrugs slightly and says, "Maybe he just cheated on you." Monica's jaw drops slightly.
The setting changes to a view of city buildings at night.
The scene changes to Jason and Phoebe walking along a brick wall covered in posters at night, both holding paper cups.
Jason looks at Phoebe as they walk and says, "And I know I'll never get rich doing it, but I got to tell you, it's pretty cool knowing that you're making a difference in a kid's"
Jason says, "life." Phoebe smiles and replies, "That is so great."
They stop walking as smoke begins to billow from the hood of a car parked on the street.

Flames erupt from the car's hood. Jason exclaims, "Oh, my God." Phoebe also says, "Oh, my God." Jason pulls Phoebe's arm and says, "We better call the fire department." Phoebe pulls back and says, "No,"
Phoebe frees her arm from Jason's grasp.
Phoebe gestures with her cup and says, "we don't need a fireman. We need, like, a good mechanic."
Jason looks at Phoebe.
Jason asks, "What?" Phoebe gestures toward the car and replies, "Well, it's probably just really low on coolant."
A siren is heard in the distance. Phoebe jumps slightly and turns toward the sound.
Phoebe smiles widely and says, "Oh, my God. Here they come."
Jason grabs her arm again and says, "But we got to get out of here."
Phoebe pulls back and argues, "Why? Look, if I wanted to see a fireman, I would date one, okay?" Jason pulls her away, and they run off-screen.
The scene changes to an examination room. Ross sits on an exam table while Rhodes, wearing a white doctor's coat, stands nearby looking at a file.
Rhodes reads from the file and says, "That's all it is. A third nipple."
Ross smiles, nodding slightly, and says, "Just your run of the mill third nipple."
Ross points over his shoulder to his back and asks, "You can take it off."
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "Just slice that baby right off."
Rhodes closes the file and says, "Take your shirt off and let's see what we're dealing with here."
Ross stands up and begins unbuttoning his shirt. Rhodes turns away and puts on a pair of gloves.
Ross lowers his pants slightly. Rhodes turns back toward him and asks, "What are you doing?"
Ross continues to lower his pants.
Ross turns his back to Rhodes, pulling his shirt up and his pants down further. Ross says, "Just showing you my run of the mill, slice it right off third nipple."
Rhodes looks at Ross's lower back and says, "Well, that's not a third"
Rhodes gestures toward Ross and continues, "nipple. First of all, it's on your ass."
Ross turns his head to look back at Rhodes.
Ross asks, "Well, then, what is it?"
Rhodes holds his hand up, stepping closer to examine the area, and says, "Wait a minute. Hold it."
Rhodes steps away, walks to the door, opens it, and looks out into the hallway.
Rhodes calls out, "Jansen, will you come in here a moment?"
A voice from the hallway replies, "I'm with Hamilton."
Rhodes says, "He's good with weird things. Bring him in too."
Ross stands with his back to the door, holding his shirt up and pants down, his brow furrowed.
The scene changes. Chandler says, "Hey, you know what? You don't see a lot of topless tap dancing."
Chandler continues speaking as the segment ends.
In a coffee shop, Monica and Joey sit on an orange couch. Chandler sits at a table nearby holding a small glass bottle.
Joey turns his head. "I really enjoy these little talks we have."
Monica holds a blue mug and gestures with her free hand.
Monica lowers her hand. "I gotta go water Pete's plants."
Monica shifts slightly on the couch. "You know what?"
Monica continues, "If he's gonna break up with me, maybe I won't water his plants."
Chandler points at Monica with his bottle. "If he's gonna break up with you, maybe Joey and I should water his plants."
Monica smiles and takes a sip from her mug. Joey looks toward Chandler.
Chandler says, "You know what I mean?"
Joey raises his eyebrows.
Chandler gestures with his bottle. "Or we could go over there and pee on"
Phoebe and Rachel walk into the coffee shop and stop near the couch. Phoebe wears an orange shirt and Rachel wears a purple shirt.
Phoebe speaks, gesturing with her hands. "just. I can't take just."
Phoebe continues, "I'm always afraid one of them's going to catch me with the other one. It's making me crazy." Joey turns to her. "Well, hey,"
Joey says, "then why don't you break up with one of them?"
Joey gestures with his hands. "Whoa, whoa, whoa. What happened to play in the field?"
Phoebe says, "Well, it doesn't feel like playing anymore."
Phoebe gestures outward. "It feels like work. It's like I'm working in the field." Rachel sits in an armchair nearby.
Monica turns her head toward Phoebe.
Monica says, "So, Phoebe, pick one of them." Phoebe turns toward her. Monica adds, "Yeah, which one do you like more?"
Phoebe looks downward.
Phoebe looks back up. "Well, Vince is know, because he's a guy know, he's just so burly."
Phoebe continues looking forward.
Phoebe smiles slightly. "So very burly."
Phoebe covers her mouth with her hand and smiles broadly. Joey grins.
Joey says, "Okay, good. So there you go. Go with Vince."
Phoebe crosses her arms. "Yeah, but Jason's really sensitive."
Chandler gestures with his bottle.
Chandler says, "Well, sensitive is important."
Chandler points the bottle toward Phoebe. "Pick him."
Joey waves a hand dismissively. "Yeah. Oh, sure."
Joey continues, "Go with the sissy."
Phoebe looks at Joey with a still expression.
Phoebe says, "Jason is not a" Joey raises his hands. "no, no."
Joey gestures toward Chandler. "I meant Chandler"
Chandler's face remains still. The setting changes to an examination room. Ross lies face down on a medical table, wearing a hospital gown and plaid shirt, surrounded by doctors in white coats.
Ross rests his head on his arms. The doctors converse softly in the background.
Ross lifts his head slightly and looks around at the crowd.
Ross says, "plans."
Dr. Rhodes, wearing a headlamp and clear safety glasses, looks at Ross, then straightens his posture.
Dr. Rhodes addresses the crowd, clasping his hands. "Thank you so much for coming on such a short notice."
Dr. Rhodes touches his headlamp. "Ladies and gentlemen, I've I've been practicing medicine for"
Dr. Rhodes points with both index fingers. "23 years and I'm stumped"
Dr. Rhodes gestures toward Ross. The doctors all lean forward simultaneously, crowding closely around the table.
Ross lowers his head back onto his arms as the doctors inspect his back.
The setting changes to an exterior view of city skyscrapers at dusk. The scene then changes to an upscale apartment interior. Monica, Ross, Chandler, and Joey enter.
Monica steps forward and claps her hands. "in. All right, check this out. Lights."
The room lights turn on brightly. Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Ross cover their faces. Monica says, "Whoa. All right, less light. No, bad lights. Lights."
Monica waves her hands defensively. "Go away."
Monica lowers her hands.
Joey lowers his hands. "See, you just need to find the right command."
Ross turns to a wall panel. "Yes. And the dimmer switch." He presses a button and the lights dim.
Joey points to a small screen mounted on the wall. "Whoa, for a rich guys, that's a pretty small no." Monica steps past him. "No, that's the video"
Monica gestures widely with her arms. "phone. But hey, guys, you're not supposed to be here, so please do not touch anything."
Chandler sits down heavily on a black leather couch.
Chandler rests his arms on the back of the couch. "IKEA. This is comfortable."
Rachel and Phoebe enter the apartment.
Rachel walks forward. "This place is amazing." Phoebe steps beside her, looking around. "Oh, God, that is the nicest I know."
Rachel adds, "No, no, but it's the nicest kitchen. The refrigerator told me"
Rachel smiles. "to have a great day."
Joey stands by a desk, holding an open binder.
Joey holds the binder out. "Look at this. Millionaire's checkbook." Monica turns toward him. "Joey, put that down."
An electronic ringing sound begins.
Monica raises her hands quickly. "Oh, my God. That's Pete. Okay, get out."
Monica hurries toward the small wall screen. "How the hell do you answer a video phone?"
A man's voice sounds from the screen. "Monica."
The others scatter. Joey runs toward the back. Chandler dives behind the couch.
Monica stands in front of the screen. "I guess that's how." A man's face appears on the screen. "Hey, Monica. How's it going?"
Monica clasps her hands together. "Oh, it's good."
Monica smiles slightly.
Monica says, "It's good. Just here watering the plants."
The man on the screen says, "Well, don't forget that ficus over there by Rachel."
Rachel crouches near a bookcase.
The man on the screen says, "The Chandler's on the couch."
Chandler lies flat on his stomach on the couch cushions.
Chandler raises his head slightly. "Yeah, I see him."
The man on the screen smiles. "You guys are just the worst hiders ever."
Monica looks toward Chandler as the segment ends.
In an apartment living room, Monica stands facing a small television built into the wooden wall paneling. On the screen, a man is visible.
Monica leans closer to the screen and says, "Pete, the other day when you said you needed to talk just so I know,"
"is it good news or bad news?" she asks. The man on the screen looks at her.
The man on the screen says, "Oh, it's good news. No, it's definitely good news. Hold on a second, I have another call." He points a remote control toward the camera.
Monica, still facing the screen, smiles and asks, "Hey, how's it going?"
Monica says, "No, it's still me." Audience laughter is heard.
The man on the screen replies, "No, it's not. I've got picture and picture," holding up a small black device.
Monica looks away from the screen momentarily, then looks back. Behind her, Chandler, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe watch.
The man on the screen says, "Yeah."
The man on the screen says, "Yeah. Okay. I'm going to have to call you back later."
Monica slightly parts her lips.
The man on the screen says, "Monica, you I'm going to have to call you back." Monica nods.
Monica places her hand on her chest.
Monica says, "Okay, so I'll see you soon. Okay, I love you."

The man on the screen says, "I love you." Monica replies, "I love you," and turns toward the group in the living room. Chandler sits on the back of a black leather sofa, with Joey, Rachel, Phoebe, and Ross standing nearby. They all repeat the phrase in unison.
Audience laughter sounds as Monica walks toward the group, smiling. "Okay. Well, it's good news," she says, clasping her hands.
"Good news," Monica repeats, resting her hand on her arm. She asks, "So what do you think the good news is?"
Joey, sitting at a desk in the background, looks at an open checkbook. "Wow, look at this. He wrote a check for," Joey says.
Joey continues, "$50,000 to Hugo Lindgren's Ring Design." Monica stares at him, her arms by her side.
Joey looks up from the checkbook, realizing his mistake as audience laughter plays.
"Oh, sorry," Joey says. He gestures with his hand and adds, "What do you think the good news is?"
The group stares at Joey in silence amid laughter. Monica slowly raises her hand to her chest. Phoebe and Rachel look at each other.
Phoebe smiles widely and says, "Monica's gonna marry a millionaire," while shaking her hands near her face. Rachel covers her mouth.
Ross walks toward Monica holding a black cylindrical lampshade. "You gotta get mom on the phone. Call mom. Call mom," he says, handing the lampshade to Monica. She takes it and says, "Hello."
The television screen now shows an older woman looking forward.
Monica looks at the screen and says, "And that's beach."
Monica says, "Mom," speaking into the lampshade. In the background, Chandler falls backward over the back of the sofa, drawing loud audience laughter.
The older woman on the screen stares straight ahead with a neutral expression.

The scene changes to an exterior shot of a multi-story brick apartment building on a city street corner. Yellow taxis pass by. The view then shifts inside an apartment kitchen as Monica enters, followed by Rachel and Chandler.
Rachel gestures with both hands as she walks alongside Monica. "You definitely have to make it a theme wedding. And the theme could be look how much money we've got," Rachel says.
Monica stops and looks at Rachel.
Rachel says, "You know? I mean, you could put, you could put money in in the invitation."
"You you could have, like, little money place settings," Rachel adds. Chandler grabs a bottle from the refrigerator.
Rachel points her finger. "And you could start with a money salad."
"I mean, it'll be dry, but people will like it," Rachel says. Monica turns toward her.
Monica holds a bottle and says, "Could you stop?" Audience laughter is heard.
Monica unscrews the cap of the bottle.
"We've only been going out a couple of weeks," Monica says.
"I mean, we don't even know if he's going to propose," Monica adds, looking at Rachel.
Rachel steps closer. "Yes, but this is Pete, okay? He's not like other people. On your first date, he," she says.
Chandler sits at the kitchen table holding a bottle. "took you to Rome. For most guys, that's like a third or fourth date kind of thing," Chandler says, gesturing with his hand. Laughter sounds.
Monica gestures with both hands, looking around the room.
"Well, if that's what it is, then it's crazy," Monica says.
Ross, standing near the counter with Joey, says, "Monica's right. We're talking about getting married here."
Ross continues, "Okay? She can't just rush into this."
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "What do you know? You married a lesbian." Audience laughter erupts.
Ross stares at Rachel, his expression stiffening. Joey looks between Ross and Rachel.
Joey smirks and nods slightly. Ross turns his gaze away.
Joey says, "Yeah, you could do worse. Monica, he's a really good guy." Laughter plays.
Phoebe, standing in the living area, walks toward Monica. "You know what you should do? Okay, don't decide now, okay?" Phoebe says.
Phoebe gestures with her hands. "Just wait till you're in the moment, and whatever you're feeling, that will be your answer," she says.
Rachel sits at the table and taps it. "Yeah, and when Pete gets down on his knee and," she says.
Rachel gestures with her hand, "pulls out that pumpkin sized diamond ring." Phoebe looks at her watch as laughter sounds.
Phoebe steps back, touching her wrist.
Phoebe says, "All right, I got to go. I have to break up with Vince." She turns to walk away. Chandler says, "Oh, so you go?" Audience laughter is audible.
The screen fades to black.
The screen remains black until the segment ends.
